results.question: List suppliers and their nation.

JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
ORDER BY s.s_name;

results.question: Show all nations and which region they belong to.

results.question: How many customers are in each nation?

FROM customer c
JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
GROUP BY n.n_name
ORDER BY n.n_name;
results.gold_sql: SELECT n.n_name, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customer c JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey GROUP BY n.n_name ORDER BY customer_count DESC;

results.question: What is the total revenue per region?
results.generated_sql:
WITH regional_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    r.r_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
)
SELECT r_name, revenue FROM regional_revenue ORDER BY revenue DESC;
results.gold_sql: SELECT r.r_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name ORDER BY revenue DESC;

GROUP BY n.n_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

results.question: What is the average discount per region?
results.generated_sql:
WITH discount_by_region AS (
  SELECT 
    r.r_name,
    AVG(l.promo_reduction) as avg_discount
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
)
SELECT r_name, ROUND(avg_discount, 4) as avg_discount
FROM discount_by_region
ORDER BY avg_discount DESC;
results.gold_sql: SELECT r.r_name, AVG(l.promo_reduction) AS avg_discount FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name ORDER BY avg_discount DESC;

results.question: Which supplier has the most parts in the region with the lowest revenue?
results.generated_sql:
WITH supplier_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    s.s_suppkey,
    s.s_name,
    r.r_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as supplier_revenue
  FROM supplier s
  JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  JOIN partsupp ps ON s.s_suppkey = ps.ps_suppkey
  JOIN lineitem l ON ps.ps_partkey = l.l_partkey AND ps.ps_suppkey = l.l_suppkey
  GROUP BY s.s_suppkey, s.s_name, r.r_name
),
region_revenue AS (
  SELECT r_name, SUM(supplier_revenue) as total_revenue
  FROM supplier_revenue
  GROUP BY r_name
),
lowest_revenue_region AS (
  SELECT r_name
  FROM region_revenue
  ORDER BY total_revenue ASC
  LIMIT 1
),
supplier_parts_in_region AS (
  SELECT 
    sr.s_suppkey,
    sr.s_name,
    sr.r_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ps.ps_partkey) as part_count
  FROM supplier_revenue sr
  JOIN partsupp ps ON sr.s_suppkey = ps.ps_suppkey
  JOIN lineitem l ON ps.ps_partkey = l.l_partkey AND ps.ps_suppkey = l.l_suppkey
  WHERE sr.r_name = (SELECT r_name FROM lowest_revenue_region)
  GROUP BY sr.s_suppkey, sr.s_name, sr.r_name
)
SELECT 
  s_name,
  part_count
FROM supplier_parts_in_region
ORDER BY part_count DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.gold_sql: WITH regional_revenue AS (SELECT r.r_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name), lowest_region AS (SELECT r_name FROM regional_revenue ORDER BY revenue ASC LIMIT 1) SELECT s.s_name, COUNT(DISTINCT ps.ps_partkey) AS part_count FROM supplier s JOIN partsupp ps ON s.s_suppkey = ps.ps_suppkey JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey JOIN lowest_region lr ON r.r_name = lr.r_name GROUP BY s.s_name ORDER BY part_count DESC LIMIT 1;

results.question: Find the top 3 nations by revenue where the average discount is above 0.05.
results.generated_sql:
WITH nation_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    n.n_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue,
    AVG(l.promo_reduction) as avg_discount
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN supplier s ON l.l_suppkey = s.s_suppkey
  JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  GROUP BY n.n_name
),
filtered_nations AS (
  SELECT n_name, revenue, avg_discount
  FROM nation_revenue
  WHERE avg_discount > 0.05
)
SELECT n_name, revenue, avg_discount
FROM filtered_nations
ORDER BY revenue DESC
LIMIT 3;
results.gold_sql: SELECT n.n_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue, AVG(l.promo_reduction) AS avg_discount FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey GROUP BY n.n_name HAVING AVG(l.promo_reduction) > 0.05 ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 3;

results.question: List customers who have placed more orders than the average number of orders per customer.
results.generated_sql:
WITH customer_order_counts AS (
  SELECT o.o_custkey, COUNT(DISTINCT o.o_orderkey) as order_count
  FROM orders o
  GROUP BY o.o_custkey
),
avg_orders AS (
  SELECT AVG(order_count) as avg_order_count
  FROM customer_order_counts
)
SELECT coc.o_custkey, c.c_name, coc.order_count
FROM customer_order_counts coc
JOIN customer c ON coc.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
CROSS JOIN avg_orders ao
WHERE coc.order_count > ao.avg_order_count
ORDER BY coc.order_count DESC;
results.gold_sql: WITH customer_orders AS (SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count FROM customer c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name), avg_orders AS (SELECT AVG(order_count) AS avg_count FROM customer_orders) SELECT co.c_name, co.order_count FROM customer_orders co CROSS JOIN avg_orders ao WHERE co.order_count > ao.avg_count ORDER BY co.order_count DESC;

results.question: Which region has the highest average order value?
results.generated_sql:
WITH region_order_values AS (

  FROM orders o
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey

results.question: Find the supplier with the highest revenue in each region.
results.generated_sql:
WITH supplier_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    s.s_suppkey,
    s.s_name,
    r.r_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue
  FROM supplier s
  JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  JOIN partsupp ps ON s.s_suppkey = ps.ps_suppkey
  JOIN lineitem l ON ps.ps_partkey = l.l_partkey AND ps.ps_suppkey = l.l_suppkey
  GROUP BY s.s_suppkey, s.s_name, r.r_name
),
ranked_suppliers AS (
  SELECT 
    s_suppkey,
    s_name,
    r_name,
    revenue,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY r_name ORDER BY revenue DESC) as rank
  FROM supplier_revenue
)
SELECT 
  r_name,
  s_name,
  revenue
FROM ranked_suppliers
WHERE rank = 1
ORDER BY r_name;
results.gold_sql: WITH supplier_revenue AS (SELECT r.r_name, s.s_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY r.r_name ORDER BY SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) DESC) AS rn FROM lineitem l JOIN supplier s ON l.l_suppkey = s.s_suppkey JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name, s.s_name) SELECT r_name, s_name, revenue FROM supplier_revenue WHERE rn = 1;

results.question: What percentage of total revenue comes from each region?
results.generated_sql:
WITH regional_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    r.r_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as region_revenue
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
),
total_revenue AS (
  SELECT SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as overall_revenue
  FROM lineitem l
)
SELECT 
  rr.r_name,
  rr.region_revenue,
  tr.overall_revenue,
  ROUND(100.0 * rr.region_revenue / tr.overall_revenue, 2) as percentage
FROM regional_revenue rr
CROSS JOIN total_revenue tr
ORDER BY rr.region_revenue DESC;
results.gold_sql: WITH total AS (SELECT SUM(total_value * (1 - promo_reduction)) AS grand_total FROM lineitem), by_region AS (SELECT r.r_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name) SELECT br.r_name, br.revenue, ROUND((br.revenue / t.grand_total) * 100, 2) AS pct_of_total FROM by_region br CROSS JOIN total t ORDER BY pct_of_total DESC;
results.match_details: Mismatch: gold has 5 rows x 3 cols (['r_name', 'revenue', 'pct_of_total']), agent has 5 rows x 4 cols (['r_name', 'region_revenue', 'overall_revenue', 'percentage'])

results.question: Which nation has the highest revenue among high-risk customers?
results.generated_sql:
WITH high_risk_customers AS (
  SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, c.churn_risk, n.n_name
  FROM customer c
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  WHERE c.churn_risk = 'High'
),
customer_revenue AS (
  SELECT hrc.c_custkey, hrc.n_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue
  FROM high_risk_customers hrc
  JOIN orders o ON hrc.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
  JOIN lineitem l ON o.o_orderkey = l.l_orderkey
  GROUP BY hrc.c_custkey, hrc.n_name
)
SELECT n_name, SUM(revenue) as total_revenue
FROM customer_revenue
GROUP BY n_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.gold_sql: SELECT n.n_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey WHERE c.churn_risk = 'HIGH_RISK' GROUP BY n.n_name ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 1;

results.question: Show the top 5 customers by revenue in the ASIA region.
results.generated_sql:
WITH customer_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    c.c_custkey,
    c.c_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue
  FROM customer c
  JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
  JOIN lineitem l ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey

  WHERE r.r_name = 'ASIA'

)
SELECT c_name, revenue
FROM customer_revenue
ORDER BY revenue DESC
LIMIT 5;

results.question: Compare average account balance between high-risk and low-risk customers by region.
results.generated_sql:
WITH customer_risk AS (
  SELECT 
    c.c_custkey,
    c.c_name,
    c.c_acctbal,
    c.churn_risk,
    n.n_name
  FROM customer c
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
),
high_risk AS (
  SELECT 
    c_custkey,
    c_name,
    c_acctbal,
    n_name
  FROM customer_risk
  WHERE churn_risk = 'high'
),
low_risk AS (
  SELECT 
    c_custkey,
    c_name,
    c_acctbal,
    n_name
  FROM customer_risk
  WHERE churn_risk = 'low'
)
SELECT 
  'High-Risk' as risk_level,
  r.r_name as region,
  AVG(hr.c_acctbal) as avg_account_balance
FROM high_risk hr
JOIN nation n ON hr.n_name = n.n_name
JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
GROUP BY r.r_name
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'Low-Risk' as risk_level,
  r.r_name as region,
  AVG(lr.c_acctbal) as avg_account_balance
FROM low_risk lr
JOIN nation n ON lr.n_name = n.n_name
JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
GROUP BY r.r_name
ORDER BY risk_level, region;
results.gold_sql: SELECT r.r_name, c.churn_risk, AVG(c.c_acctbal) AS avg_balance FROM customer c JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey WHERE c.churn_risk IN ('HIGH_RISK', 'LOW_RISK') GROUP BY r.r_name, c.churn_risk ORDER BY r.r_name, c.churn_risk;

results.question: What is the month-over-month revenue trend for 1995?
results.generated_sql:
WITH monthly_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) as month,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.o_orderdate) = 1995
  GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate)
)
SELECT 
  month,
  revenue,
  LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) as prev_month_revenue,
  revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) as revenue_change
FROM monthly_revenue
ORDER BY month;
results.gold_sql: WITH monthly AS (SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate) AS month, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.o_orderdate) = 1995 GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM o.o_orderdate)) SELECT month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_revenue, ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) * 100, 2) AS mom_pct_change FROM monthly ORDER BY month;

results.question: Show the year-over-year revenue growth rate.
results.generated_sql:
WITH yearly_revenue AS (

    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.o_orderdate) as year,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as revenue

)

results.question: Show each region's revenue and its difference from the overall average.
results.generated_sql:
WITH regional_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    r.r_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as region_revenue
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
),
overall_avg AS (
  SELECT AVG(region_revenue) as avg_revenue
  FROM regional_revenue
)
SELECT 
  rr.r_name,
  rr.region_revenue,
  oa.avg_revenue,
  rr.region_revenue - oa.avg_revenue as difference_from_avg
FROM regional_revenue rr
CROSS JOIN overall_avg oa
ORDER BY rr.region_revenue DESC;
results.gold_sql: WITH region_rev AS (SELECT r.r_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name) SELECT r_name, revenue, AVG(revenue) OVER () AS avg_revenue, revenue - AVG(revenue) OVER () AS diff_from_avg FROM region_rev;

results.question: What if we increased the discount by 10%? How would that affect total revenue?
results.generated_sql:
WITH original_revenue AS (
  SELECT SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as original_value
  FROM lineitem l
),
simulated_revenue AS (
  SELECT SUM(l.total_value * (1 - (l.promo_reduction + 0.10))) as simulated_value
  FROM lineitem l
)
SELECT 
  orig.original_value,
  sim.simulated_value,
  sim.simulated_value - orig.original_value as difference,
  ROUND(((sim.simulated_value - orig.original_value) / orig.original_value) * 100, 2) as pct_change
FROM original_revenue orig
CROSS JOIN simulated_revenue sim;

results.question: Simulate a 5% price increase on all line items and show revenue by region.
results.generated_sql:

  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
),

  SELECT 
    r.r_name,

  FROM lineitem l

  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey

)

ORDER BY or_rev.r_name;

results.question: What if we increased the price by 5% and reduced the discount by 3%? Show revenue by region.
results.generated_sql:
WITH original_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    r.r_name,
    SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) as original_revenue
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
),
simulated_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    r.r_name,
    SUM(
      CASE 
        WHEN r.r_name = 'EUROPE' THEN l.total_value * 1.05 * (1 - (l.promo_reduction * 0.97))
        ELSE l.total_value * 1.05 * (1 - l.promo_reduction)
      END
    ) as simulated_revenue
  FROM lineitem l
  JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
  JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
  JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
  JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey
  GROUP BY r.r_name
)
SELECT 
  or_rev.r_name,
  or_rev.original_revenue,
  sim_rev.simulated_revenue,
  sim_rev.simulated_revenue - or_rev.original_revenue as difference,
  ROUND(((sim_rev.simulated_revenue - or_rev.original_revenue) / or_rev.original_revenue) * 100, 2) as pct_change
FROM original_revenue or_rev
JOIN simulated_revenue sim_rev ON or_rev.r_name = sim_rev.r_name
ORDER BY or_rev.r_name;
results.gold_sql: WITH original AS (SELECT r.r_name, SUM(l.total_value * (1 - l.promo_reduction)) AS original_revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name), simulated AS (SELECT r.r_name, SUM(l.total_value * 1.05 * (1 - GREATEST(l.promo_reduction * 0.97, 0.0))) AS simulated_revenue FROM lineitem l JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey JOIN customer c ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey JOIN nation n ON c.c_nationkey = n.n_nationkey JOIN region r ON n.n_regionkey = r.r_regionkey GROUP BY r.r_name) SELECT o.r_name, o.original_revenue, s.simulated_revenue, s.simulated_revenue - o.original_revenue AS difference, ROUND(((s.simulated_revenue - o.original_revenue) / o.original_revenue) * 100, 2) AS pct_change FROM original o JOIN simulated s ON o.r_name = s.r_name ORDER BY o.r_name;

results.question: How sensitive is total revenue to discount changes? Test discount increases of 5%, 10%, 15%, and 20%.
results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  discount_increase,
  SUM(l.total_value * (1 - (l.promo_reduction + discount_increase / 100))) as revenue_with_discount_increase
FROM lineitem l
GROUP BY discount_increase
ORDER BY discount_increase;
